Example: Perching  
Can we make a ﬁxed-wing UAV land on a perch like a bird?

Experiment Design  
• Glider (no propellor) 
• Flat plate wings 
• Dihedral (passive roll stability)  
• Offboard sensing and control

• Linear actuator model (+ saturations, delay)  
• Real ﬂight data (no wind tunnel)

Feedback is essential...  
•  to compensate for initial condition errors, disturbances, and imperfect 
model 
•  agile airplanes are open loop unstable 
open loop  
feedback  
14

Today’s goal  
Use systems theory to gain insight into how to control a system.  
15

What causes these diﬀerent types of responses?  
16

Structure of a Control Problem  
(Simple) Control systems have three parts.  
The plant is the system to be controlled.  
The sensor measures the output of the plant.  
The controller speciﬁes a command C to the plant based on the  
diﬀerence between the input X and sensor output S.

Destabilizing Eﬀect of Delay  
Adding delay in the feedback loop makes it more diﬃcult to stabilize. 
Ideal sensor: ds[n] = do[n] 
More realistic sensor (with delay): ds[n] = do[n − 1] 
1
Re z
Im z
1

Im z
Fastest response without delay: single pole at z = 0.

Destabilizing Eﬀect of Delay  
Adding more delay in the feedback loop is even worse. 
More realistic sensor (with delay): ds[n] = do[n − 1] 
Even more delay: ds[n] = do[n − 2]

53

Feedback and Control: Summary  
Feedback is an elegant way to design a control system.  
Stability of a feedback system is determined by its dominant pole.  
Delays tend to decrease the stability of a feedback system.  
54
